attacking_style: Attacks inside final 3km on summit finishes to bank time before time trials where he's vulnerable. Prefers solo accelerations at sustained high tempo rather than explosive surges, often on climbs of 10%+ gradient.
team_tactics: INEOS sets relentless tempo through domestiques before Bernal attacks late; teammates sacrificed for him even over former Tour winners (Thomas). On descents, team allows splits to form forcing rivals to chase. Heavily protected in positioning throughout stages.
terrain_preference: Excels on high-altitude climbs (gained 2:07 on Iseran) and brutal gradients (won on 14% gravel finish). Exposed on time trials (lost minutes to specialists repeatedly), gravel handling unstable, and vulnerable in crosswinds/echelon formations.
vulnerability: Chronic back problems causing DNS/form collapse (2020-2023). Cannot sustain initial attacks on longest climbs when covered by Roglič. Poor positioning instincts on flat stages leads to caught-behind-splits pattern. Crash susceptibility documented across multiple Tours.
key_patterns: Attacks 3km from summit finishes to gain time before ITTs (4+ observations across Grand Tours)
Back problems recur causing race abandonment or catastrophic form loss (2020 DNS, 7:19 loss, 2023 struggles)
Initiates attacks first but fades when rivals counter, especially Roglič (5+ Vuelta observations)
Caught behind crosswind/echelon splits showing flat-stage positioning weakness (3 documented instances)
Descends aggressively post-2023 injury, attacking solo on descents refusing collaboration (Dauphine 2023)
Team sacrifices even former Tour winners for his support, revealing designated leadership status (Thomas domestique work)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 shows continued crash susceptibility and significantly delayed recovery from 2022 injury. Struggles on successive climbs despite renewed descending confidence. Power insufficient compared to pre-crash baseline.
